items.instruction: Which entity must receive explosive cargo information during an emergency landing?
items.question_text: Explosive-cargo emergency landing notifications include which recipients?

choices.text: The emergency equipment crew, the airport management, and the appropriate military agencies when requested by the pilot.
The tower controller handling the emergency landing, with no other notifications.
The ARTCC, which then handles all downstream notifications.
The RCC, because explosive cargo is a search-and-rescue matter.

items.explanation: Relay explosive-cargo information to the emergency equipment crew, airport management, and appropriate military agencies when requested by the pilot.

items.question_text: Under 10-5-2, in the event of a debris-generating space launch or reentry vehicle mishap, what should be issued to affected aircraft?

choices.text: An alert broadcast to all affected aircraft informing them of the mishap and, if known, the approximate location of the debris fall area or activated DRA.
A routine ATIS update only, since debris alerts are handled through automated broadcasts.
Individual calls to each aircraft, because group broadcasts are not authorized for debris mishap events.
No immediate action — wait for the vehicle operator to provide exact debris coordinates before issuing any advisories.

items.explanation: Per 10-5-2a, in the event of a debris-generating space launch or reentry vehicle mishap, issue an alert broadcast to all affected aircraft informing them of the mishap and, if known, the approximate location of the debris fall area or the activated DRA.
